findings.area: agenda row — onsdag 15. juli, 21:00 England – Argentina
findings.issue: Fixture shows a flag emoji next to 'Argentina' but no flag next to 'England', so only one side of the matchup is badged. Same asymmetry visible at 375/393px. Names are still fully legible, but the flag treatment looks inconsistent compared to other fixtures.

notes: No horizontal scroll or edge clipping at 375px; single quiet column holds at all three widths.

Fixture and event names read in full, nothing truncated: 'Genesis Scottish Open', 'ISCO Championship', 'Tour de France 2026', 'Wimbledon 2026: Herrefinale', 'VM-semifinale 1', 'England – Argentina', 'The 154th Open Championship', 'Corales Puntacana Championship', 'London Athletics Meet – Karsten Warholm (400m hekk)', 'Round 1 – XXVI Obert Internacional Sant Martí 2026', 'VM-bronsefinale'.
Norwegian-channel contract holds: only Viaplay, TV 2 Play, TV 2, NRK1/NRK TV, NRK 1, Max +1 appear as 'where to watch' — no ESPN/FOX/Sky at any width.

One row (Round 1 – XXVI Obert Internacional Sant Martí 2026) exercises the honest empty '–' channel placeholder — renders quietly, not alarming, and is easy to distinguish from a missing/broken value.
Round labels (Semifinale, Bronsefinale) show correctly next to knockout fixtures at all widths.

Must-see accent (left border + tinted wash) is selective, not universal: unaccented rows (Tour de France, Wimbledon Herrefinale, the chess row, VM-bronsefinale) sit alongside accented ones — consistent with the prior review's finding that this ties to tracked favorites/Norwegian interest rather than being blanket-applied, even though more than half of today's events happen to be accented (a heavy day for Norwegian golf/tennis/athletics, not a design regression).

Header, 'Dine neste' tracked list, day-group headings, 'Hva vi følger'/'Rediger' links, and footer (Oppdatert / Abonner i kalender / last ned / Aktivitet / AI-budsjett) intact and aligned at every width.
Could not assess clearly: the small icon/arrow next to the clock in the header (top right) — unclear what it represents, not clipped or broken.
